notes: User-action framing of the same pi behavior as the interrupted-turn scenario (both share coverage_id user-esc-interrupt). User cancels a long in-flight pi turn with Escape, then issues a recovery prompt that proves the parser reset. The user-observable signal is ready→working→ready with state NOT sticking in working post-interrupt, plus a clean recovery arc on the same rollout session. IMPORTANT pi-specific structural nuance (see assessment.json): pi's parser emits turn_done ONLY on stopReason=stop, and an ESC-cancelled turn never reaches stopReason=stop, so the interrupted turn produces NO turn_done; pi's parser also never sets LastWasUserInterrupt, so the classifier's rule-3 'user ESC interrupt → ready' branch does not fire for pi. Consequently the interrupt itself emits NO standalone working→ready transition — the session stays working after Escape until the RECOVERY prompt's reply lands a stopReason=stop, which settles working→ready via 'agent finished turn → ready'. The spec therefore asserts the working flip at turn 1 and the eventual settle to ready that the recovery turn carries; it does NOT assert a distinct interrupt-triggered transition (pi does not emit one). C7 / SESSION-IDENTITY (same workaround that made multi-turn-conversation and interrupted-turn pass): the live REPL emits a transient proc-<PID> pre-session born ready at launch, so session_birth — which only asserts 'a session appears in ready within 2s of launch' — legitimately matches that pre-session. The TURN ARCS must land on the UUID rollout session: t1_start anchors to recording start with NO session pin so the matcher picks the FIRST working in the whole recording, which is necessarily the rollout session (the pre-session never goes working); every later phase, the pid_bind, and the teardown then pin to t1_start's session via same_session_as so a co-occurring pre-session can never satisfy them. The driver's interrupt step sends Escape and decrements its EXPECTED_TURNS (the cancelled turn yields no stop), so the recovery send+wait_turn pair stays in sync — the recipe does NOT follow interrupt directly with wait_turn. HOW runs differ: exact assistant wording (the cancelled essay text), per-turn token counts, how much of the essay streamed before Escape landed, and sub-second timing all vary (model-output variance); pi may emit additional mid-turn assistant bursts before the cancel. Stable structurally: at least one ready→working→ready arc on one rollout session_id that does NOT stick in working and never enters waiting, a single process_exited, and a final ready state.

text: A session appears in ready within 2s of REPL launch
First prompt (long essay) → working (binds the rollout session; the pre-session never enters working)
PID bound to the rollout session
After the Escape interrupt the turn carries no turn_done; the recovery prompt's reply settles the rollout session back to ready — state does not stick in working, and never entered waiting
REPL torn down once after the recovery turn → a single process_exited on the rollout session; state immediately before teardown is ready
